bullets: Hook execution telemetry now writes JSONL files under `~/claude-analytics/hook-logs/` instead of `~/.claude/hooks.db`. Three files: `invocations.jsonl`, `surface-lessons-context.jsonl`, `session-start-context.jsonl`. The invocation row embeds the full hook stdin as a parsed object (or string fallback when stdin failed JSON parse) — previously only extracted fields were persisted. Toolkit data moves out of `~/.claude/` and into the shared `~/claude-analytics/` namespace.

`surface-lessons.sh` intra-session dedup still reads `hooks.db.surface_lessons_context`, now read-only. The claude-sessions indexer projects JSONL rows into the table (~1min cadence). Accepted tradeoff: a lesson may re-surface within one ingestion window in exchange for standardizing how downstream consumers ingest hook telemetry.

Settings: new `CLAUDE_ANALYTICS_HOOKS_DIR` env var (default `$HOME/claude-analytics/hook-logs`) for the JSONL write path. `CLAUDE_ANALYTICS_HOOKS_DB` retained as a read-only override for surface-lessons dedup. `CLAUDE_TOOLKIT_TRACEABILITY` description updated to mention JSONL writes.
